reflection:
881 calories on a 21,000 step travel day. That's the cascade you already know. You flew UK to Slovenia, hauled through airports, ate almost nothing, then asked your body to fall asleep at 11pm. It said no, then woke you at 4am to make sure you got the message. Recovery this morning is 48, HRV 30, which honestly given the day before is not as bad as you feel.

The CBT thought that came in, the one about aging and losing the drive and not getting hard, didn't come from nowhere. It came from a body in a deep deficit at 4am with no glucose on board. Brains in famine don't write kind things about themselves. The reframe you wrote, the part about something that lives inside you and gets drunk when immunity drops, that's clinical thinking. Not catastrophizing. Hold on to that frame, it's the right one. The intensity didn't drop in the moment but the work was real.

Filip felt off and your dad came for money. Both of those land harder when you're depleted. Same pattern with your dad, you saw it as it happened, that's progress.

One thing for today: eat 2000 calories before noon. Front-load it. Your body needs to know it's not in famine. Then everything else gets easier to think about.

Green Slovenia, Cosita kids, mouse acquired. Today is a different day.
opener: 881 cal on a 21k step day. That's the cascade you know.
focus_for_today: Eat 2000 calories before noon. Front-load it. Stop the famine signal.
